D8 AnthropologyL3 ApplyReference

Ultramarine from Lapis — Cennini's Pasta Method

Cennini's 1390 recipe: knead ground lapis with wax + resin + linseed pasta, then leach into lye; first wash = purest blue (Virgin's mantle), third wash = grey "ultramarine ash"

Lazurite Na₈[Al₆Si₆O₂₄]S_n contains a colour-centre — an S₃⁻ radical anion trapped in a sodalite cage. Crushing lapis alone gives muddy purple because calcite and pyrite impurities dilute it; Cennini's pasta selectively wets out the heavier blue particles in 3 sequential leachings, separating purest → mid → ash grades.

Pick wash number 1–3.
